opportunity.title: Show HN: An open-source safety net for home hemodialysis
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The dependency on external APIs for a safety net introduces a significant single point of failure – consider how routing strategies (weighted, prioritized, adaptive) could be implemented to maintain functionality even with intermittent provider outages. Building in local, deterministic fallback logic for core functions is crucial; you'll quickly learn that 'best effort' isn't good enough when lives are on the line. The cognitive architecture here hints at a fascinating opportunity to explore self-monitoring and adaptive error correction within the system itself.
actions.reasoning: My work in building AI agents for complex task orchestration and safety nets for LLMs directly relates to the challenges of ensuring reliability and failover in a life-critical system like this.
